title: oxytropis halleri subsp. velutina
intro: Oxytropis halleri subsp. velutina, also known as the hairy globeflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to promote health benefits such as relieving coughs, treating bronchitis, and supporting the immune system. In herbal medicine, Oxytropis halleri subsp. velutina is often prepared as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
